channel.title: Github-Actions on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Github-Actions on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix failing Actions checks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you ask Copilot coding agent to investigate and fix failing GitHub Actions checks on a pull request directly from the mobile app. The feature starts from a failed check and uses Copilot cloud agent to work on the issue.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Xcode 27 runner image now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub-hosted macOS runners now support Xcode 27 in public preview, letting you build and test Apple apps against the latest Xcode version. This is an early-access runner image release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: setup-java v5.5.0: signature verification, Kona JDK, and Maven fixes
item.description:
setup-java v5.5.0 adds cryptographic signature verification for downloaded JDKs, support for Kona JDK, and several Maven-related fixes. It is the release over v5.4.0.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot CLI no longer needs a personal access token in GitHub Actions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI can now run in GitHub Actions with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN. A personal access token is no longer required for this workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: MCP Beyond the Chat Window: Build Diagnostics in CI
item.description:
Microsoft’s .NET blog describes using Model Context Protocol (MCP) tools for build diagnostics in CI, including the full Binlog MCP toolset and how it runs in a GitHub Actions workflow. It also reports evaluation data on efficiency gains from this approach.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Read-only Actions cache for untrusted triggers
item.description:
GitHub Actions now uses read-only cache tokens on the default branch for workflow events that can be triggered without repository write permissions. This narrows cache access for untrusted triggers by applying least privilege.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More control over your GitHub-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions org admins can now control access to GitHub-hosted runners more tightly. They can disable standard hosted-runner labels like ubuntu-latest and add custom labels for finer-grained usage control.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions steps can now be run in parallel
item.description:
GitHub Actions now supports running workflow steps concurrently by running them in the background. Previously, steps always executed sequentially, with each step waiting for the prior one to finish.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Red Hat Enterprise Linux runner images are now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub-hosted larger runners now support Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 and 10 images in public preview. The images were introduced in partnership with Red Hat and are available for organizations to use.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Actions: Build custom images from custom images
item.description:
GitHub Actions custom images now support building one custom image from another. This adds flexibility for composing and managing image-generation pipelines for GitHub-hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Safer pull_request_target defaults for GitHub Actions checkout
item.description:
GitHub is changing the default behavior of the Actions checkout action for workflows triggered by pull_request_target to reduce common security mistakes. The goal is to avoid exposing base-branch credentials or secrets to untrusted pull request code.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Control who and what triggers GitHub Actions workflows
item.description:
GitHub Actions now has workflow execution protections in public preview for GitHub Enterprise, organizations, and repositories. Administrators can define allow lists to control which users or events can trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Minimum version enforcement timeline for self-hosted runners
item.description:
GitHub Actions is resuming enforcement of minimum version requirements for self-hosted runners on github.com and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with Data Residency. The post says this is part of a broader effort to rebuild enforcement, but the excerpt does not include the specific minimum versions or timeline details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
item.description:
Pull requests created by github-actions[bot] can now run CI/CD workflows after a user approves them. The approval step is required as a security check before generated code is allowed to trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New runner images in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has added two new GitHub-hosted runner images for Actions in public preview. They are available to all users so workflows can be tested on the latest platforms before general release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token
item.description:
GitHub Agentic Workflows can now use the built-in GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN instead of requiring a personal access token. This removes the need to create and store a PAT for these workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Securing CI/CD in an agentic world: Claude Code Github action case
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported a prompt injection path in Claude Code GitHub Action that could expose workflow secrets under specific conditions. The post describes the attack chain, disclosure, Anthropic’s mitigation, and recommendations for securing AI-assisted CI/CD workflows.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Updates to GitHub Copilot billing and plans
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now uses usage-based billing for all users. Copilot code review also consumes GitHub Actions minutes, in addition to GitHub billing.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.25.5 improves query accuracy for GitHub Actions
item.description:
CodeQL 2.25.5 was released with query accuracy improvements for GitHub Actions, along with additional accuracy improvements across C/C++.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: One-click fixes for failing Actions with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Actions failures can now be handed to Copilot cloud agent with one click for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise subscribers. The new “Fix with Copilot” button starts an automated fix flow for failed jobs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Actions: Upcoming image migrations
item.description:
GitHub Actions has announced two upcoming hosted runner image migrations. GitHub is also transitioning to own and maintain the Arm64 images for hosted runners.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: More flexible secrets and variables for Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent now supports more flexible handling of secrets and variables when you delegate tasks to it. The agent runs in a GitHub Actions-backed development environment, and users can pass secrets and variables into that environment.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent starts 20% faster with Actions custom images
item.description:
Copilot cloud agent startup is now over 20% faster. The improvement comes from optimized runner environments built with GitHub Actions custom images.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot code review will start consuming GitHub Actions minutes on June 1, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Copilot code review will begin consuming GitHub Actions minutes starting June 1, 2026. The change applies to code review usage on pull requests.

  • Source: GitHub
